title: Nonadiabatic Theory of Phonon Magnetic Moments in Insulators and Metals
authors: Haoran Chen, Wenqin Chen, Kaijie Yang, Ting Cao, Di Xiao
abstract: We develop a nonadiabatic theory of phonon magnetic moments applicable to both insulators and metals. By relating the phonon magnetic moment to the force-velocity response of ions in a magnetic field, we derive a gauge-invariant expression using a gauge-covariant Wigner expansion. The formalism naturally separates Fermi-sea and Fermi-surface contributions and captures the full dependence on phonon frequency. In gapped systems, our theory reduces to previous adiabatic expressions in the low-frequency limit. Beyond this limit, it reveals additional contributions arising from resonant interband processes and the Fermi surface. Applying our theory to Pb$_{1-x}$Sn$_x$Te, we find that the Fermi-surface contribution substantially enhances the phonon magnetic moment, reproducing the same order of magnitude as the experimental observation. Our results provide a unified framework for describing phonon magnetic moments beyond the adiabatic regime.
summary: This paper presents a new theoretical framework for calculating phonon magnetic moments in both insulating and metallic regimes. It identifies that nonadiabatic processes and Fermi-surface contributions are essential to explain the large magnetic moments observed in experiments. The theory provides a unified approach that goes beyond the limitations of previous adiabatic models.
main_problem: Existing adiabatic theories of phonon magnetic moments fail to explain experimental observations in metals and doped semiconductors, particularly regarding the magnitude of moments and frequency-dependent effects.
main_result: The authors develop a unified nonadiabatic theory that captures both Fermi-sea and Fermi-surface contributions, successfully reproducing the Bohr magneton scale of magnetic moments observed in Pb1-xSnxTe.
method: The study employs linear response theory, a gauge-covariant Wigner expansion, and Green's function formalism with Matsubara frequency summation.
model_or_system: The theory applies to both insulators and metals, with a specific application to the topological crystalline insulator Pb1-xSnxTe using a massive anisotropic k.p model.
key_observables: Phonon magnetic moments, chiral phonon splitting, and the frequency-dependent anti-Hermitian matrix G(q, omega).
important_parameters: Phonon frequency, energy gap, Fermi level, Sn concentration, and electron linewidth.
main_assumptions: The framework uses the harmonic approximation and does not yet incorporate anharmonic phonon effects.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the mechanism of phonon coupling to magnetic fields; Figure 2 compares the new frequency-dependent formula to adiabatic theory; Figure 3 shows the dependence of contributions on the Fermi level.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of phonon magnetic moments, develops a gauge-invariant nonadiabatic formalism using Wigner expansion, applies the theory to a k.p model for Pb1-xSnxTe, and validates the results against experimental observations.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
